latestIgtvVideos.title: Phone Home Screen Organization
latestIgtvVideos.caption:
📲 You all know that I am obsessed with digital organization… So when the new iOS 14 came out I had to jump on the bandwagon and update my phone… But I DIDN’T go all out.⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
I have a system that works great for me, it reduces the amount of time that I’m on my phone, it makes me more efficient, and it calms me… Why change that? 🤷‍♀️⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
Updates are great. Systems that don’t work should definitely be updated. But systems that do work can be left alone.⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
I’ve seen hundreds of people saying they spent hours updating their phone, and hated it and changed it back. 🤦🏼‍♀️ I’ve definitely done this my fair share of times also… But now I’m confident in the systems that I put in place.⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
If a system is working for you and your family, don’t feel the pressure to change it. Feel the pressure to change the systems that are NOT working! 😌⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
Did you do the update? How are you liking it 👇⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
 ⠀
❤️ Always, Jess.⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
 ⠀
#digital #ios14 #ios #apple #iphone #digitalorganization #digitalinfluencer #homemaker #organization #organized #trend #trending #trendingnow #update #systems #selfconfidence #confidence #gutfeeling #change #debtfreecommunity #simplify #simplicity #simpleliving #organizedlife #organizationtips #organizationideas #cc #closedcaption #deafcommunity

latestIgtvVideos.title: Why Do I Climb Into a Freezer with 35 Degree Water?
latestIgtvVideos.caption:
🥶
 
.
Definitely NOT because it’s fun 😂 it’s a challenge- both mentally and physically- and pushes me to be better. If you challenge yourself to do hard things, you grow. Have difficult conversations, tackle hard projects, push in your workouts, and push your body in every way possible to be BETTER. It’s not easy, some days I can’t bring myself to climb in, but I’ve never climbed out wishing I didn’t.⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
Ice baths push me mentally in a way I never thought possible- how calm can you keep your body in ICE COLD water? But besides the challenge, ice baths also push me to focus on my breathing, allow me to connect with my body, help my muscles recover, increases endorphins, reduce stress levels, increase alertness, increases immune response, and builds on my willpower.⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
It took me years to come around to the idea, but now I try to take one almost everyday. In addition to ice baths; we also sauna, roll out, stretch, floss, scrape, cup, and use a percussive therapy device. Recovery is so important in the Haizman household!⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
What’s the hardest thing you have ever done? Hardest hike? Race? All nighter? Yoga class? 👇 Comment and brag all about your accomplishments- I want to hear!⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
❤️ Always, Jess.⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
 ⠀
#wimhof #icebath #coldbath #coldtherapy #recovery #health #fitness #workout @angigreene #girlswhoworkout #hardthings #pushyourself #meditation #breathing #hard #challenge #selflove #selfcare #diy #diyicebath #yoga #breathing #breathe #breathwork #women #womensupportingwomen #ice #iceicebaby #goals #habits #dailyhabits

latestIgtvVideos.title: How to Meal Plan
latestIgtvVideos.caption:

My 4-year-old still rear faces in her car seat. And she will until she outgrows it (meaning hits the weight or height limit). 

Most people don’t realize that rear facing is actually the safest position for kids for as long as their seat allows, because their neck and spine are still developing.

Forward facing too early puts a lot more force on their neck during a crash.

Every family makes their own choices, but for us this one felt like an easy decision.

Curious what everyone else does —
when did your kids turn forward facing?

#rearfacing #momlife #momsofinstagram #relatablemom #crunchymama

Most people spend months preparing for the baby, but very little time preparing for birth itself.

There are so many decisions to think through before you’re pregnant or early in pregnancy, from choosing your provider to understanding your birth options and knowing what matters most to you.

The more informed you are ahead of time, the more confident you’ll feel when the time comes. 🤍

Comment BIRTH and I’ll send you my free Preparing for Birth Guide!

#pregnancy #newmom #birthstory #birth #positivebirth

This was one of the coolest parts of labor for me- especially watching it back and realizing how instinctual everything was. 

In the middle of labor, I suddenly had this overwhelming urge to get down on my hands and knees. No one told me to do it- my body just knew. 

As soon as I was on my hands and knees, I realized how uncomfortable the bathroom floor was so I stood up, walked to the birthing tub and proceeded to start pushing. 

Shortly after, I was holding my baby in my arms. 

If you let it, birth has a way of letting your body just take over. 

If you’re preparing for birth and want to feel more informed and empowered, I put together a free birth guide with things I wish I knew before labor, postpartum prep, mindset tips, and more. 

Comment BIRTH & I’ll send it to you 🖤 (be sure your following or you won’t see the message!) 

#positivebirth #birthpreparation #birthplan #unmedicatedbirth #homebirth

I get asked about this swim vest every single summer, so I figured it deserved its own post. ☀️💦

When Adalynn was 3, we tried private swim lessons… and it was a complete disaster. She went from LOVING the water to being afraid to get in.

Instead of forcing it, we focused on making the pool fun again. This swim vest gave her the confidence to explore, move naturally in the water, and build her skills at her own pace.

One of my favorite features is that you can remove the floats as your child becomes more comfortable, so it grows with them instead of becoming useless after one season.

Just to be clear—I’m not saying to skip swim lessons. Every child is different, and this was simply what worked best for our family.

If you’re looking for an alternative to puddle jumpers this summer, this is the one I’d choose every time. 💙

Comment “SWIM” and I’ll send you the link! 🏊

#toddler #toddlerswimming #learningtoswim #toddlermom #momlife

One of the biggest lessons I learned as a mom was that chlorine doesn’t just rinse off the skin. 🫠

After pool days, my daughter used to deal with red, dry, itchy skin constantly — and I had no idea chlorine could continue sitting on the skin and causing irritation even after swimming.

Now I always use a chlorine-removing spray before bath time and it’s made such a difference in their skin (and no more that pool smell sticking around 🙃).

If you’re doing lots of pool days this summer, this is definitely something I wish I had known sooner.

Comment SPRAY and I’ll send you the link to my very own after swim spray: Chlorine Cleanse 🫶🏼

And share this with another mom who needs to add this to her pool bag!!

#momlife #toddlermom #swimming #summer #babyswim

The amount of time I spent preparing for my wedding compared to how much time I spent preparing for pregnancy… 😅

I researched venues, photographers, flowers, and seating charts for months.

But when it came to growing an actual human? I barely knew what questions to ask.

No one hands you a checklist before you start trying for a baby.

Not what to ask your provider.
Not what nutrients actually matter.
Not how your partner can prepare too.
Not how to think through finances, your support system, or the kind of birth experience you hope to have.

This carousel isn’t about telling you what decisions to make.

It’s about helping you realize there are decisions—and giving you a place to start asking thoughtful questions before you’re already overwhelmed.

Whether you’re hoping to conceive next month or next year, I hope this helps you feel a little more prepared. 🤍

✨ I also put together a FREE Birth Guide with the information I wish someone had shared with me before pregnancy, labor, and postpartum.

Comment BIRTH and I’ll send it straight to you. 💛

Save this for when you’re ready, and send it to someone who’s hoping to become a mom. 

#birth #marriedlife #ttc #justmarried #positivebirth
